title: Implement wizard IO and localization primitives
outOfScope: Installation wizard step orchestration and installer invocation
Calibration Wizard behavior and persisted session state
install.sh routing and legacy CLI compatibility
Changing project documentation language or adding third-party dependencies

sources.reason: Defines Work Item 4 input, output, localization, and parity requirements.
Existing locale/message conventions to preserve without coupling Wizard language to project language.

riskAssessment.reason: Interactive input handling and message parity form a safety boundary; incorrect defaults or missing placeholders could mislead operators.

executionDecision.reason: Predecessor wizard-core-detection-plan is merged, archived, closed, and origin/main is synchronized.

rawUserRequest: 用户已授权按计划串行执行全部 Work Item。继续执行 wizard-io-and-localization：完成 TTY 安全输入输出、帮助/返回/暂停/退出、颜色与可访问符号、ja/en/zh-CN 资源和 key/placeholder parity、测试、完整治理验证、独立 PR、合并、归档、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步后再进入下一个 Work Item。

intent.problem: The future Wizard needs safe, deterministic operator input/output and complete Japanese, English, and Simplified Chinese messages, but existing onboarding messages do not provide a reusable TTY-safe primitive or parity validation.
constraints: Use argument-list-safe and standard-library-only primitives; never wait for input in non-TTY mode.
Dangerous confirmation defaults to N; EOF and Ctrl+C are explicit outcomes, not implicit approval.
Wizard language is selected independently from detected project documentation language and must not silently fall back for user-visible content.
intent.rationale: A small input state model and checked message resources make safety behavior testable and let later wizard steps share consistent accessibility and localization semantics.

declaredIntent.summary: Provide reusable safe Wizard IO primitives and exact-parity ja/en/zh-CN message resources.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Language aliases and precedence resolve explicit option, environment, system locale, then ja
TTY and non-TTY input, EOF, Ctrl+C, Back/Pause/Quit/Help, and dangerous default N are explicit
ja, en, and zh-CN resources have exact key and placeholder parity without silent fallback

acceptance: Reusable IO primitives cover Y/N, selection, text input, Back, Pause, Quit, Help, EOF, Ctrl+C, TTY detection, non-color output, and accessible status symbols.
Language aliases normalize ja/ja-JP, en/en-US/en-GB, and zh/zh-CN/zh-Hans with explicit > environment > system locale > ja precedence.
Message resources for ja, en, and zh-CN have exact key and placeholder parity checked at startup and by tests; project documentation language remains independent.
Tests cover dangerous default N, non-TTY no-input behavior, EOF/Ctrl+C, all aliases, precedence, and parity.
AI Change Summary records implementation, verification, guidelines, checkpoints, known gaps, and residual risk.
guidelines: Keep dangerous actions fail-closed with explicit N defaults and no non-TTY blocking.
Use standard-library-only implementation and document public IO/localization APIs.
Require exact resource key/placeholder parity and do not silently fall back user-visible content.
Do not alter project documentation language or predecessor archive evidence.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record evidence before implementation and archival.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized serial execution of this governed Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert only this Work Item's IO/localization diff; preserve predecessor archive evidence and installer behavior.

budgetImpact.reason: Bounded standard-library implementation with resource fixtures and focused tests.
